_note: Catalog for the all-courses gallery (pages/courses.html). One entry per course shown in the gallery. `deck` resolves that deck's cover_image_url from tarot/_collection.json at render time (so thumbnails follow the deck art); `thumb` is a direct image path when a deck cover isn't the right image. Keep the id/title in step with COURSE_GROUPS in site-header.js (the dropdown menu) — this file is the gallery's data, that array is the menu's.

courses.title: The History of Tarot
courses.blurb: Six centuries, from a 1440s card game to the occult reinvention of 1781 — read closely, deck by deck.

courses.title: How the Cards Can Work
courses.blurb: A reading doesn't tell your fate — it might help you make it. The honest case, with the evidence.

courses.title: Ways to Contribute
courses.blurb: Everything here is public-domain data in one open repo. Fix or add a deck, a spread, a course — by the gentlest route that suits you.

courses.title: Reading the Cards
courses.blurb: The full anthology — why read at all, setting the intention, and the ecosystem of voices to read through.

courses.title: Intention Setting
courses.blurb: Why still read tarot after it disappointed you — not as a fate, but as a gate — and the lenses to read through.

courses.title: How the Golden Dawn Read the Tarot
courses.blurb: The most influential reading system the cards ever wore — Book T, its attributions, and the Rider-Waite-Smith deck that grew from it.

courses.title: Walking the Golden Dawn Path
courses.blurb: The esoteric voice, kept honest: each trump a gate you choose to walk through, a step on the Tree of Life. Pathworking, not prophecy.

courses.title: Why a Reading Feels So Personal
courses.blurb: The chill is real; its explanation is documented psychology, not hidden knowledge. A deep dive into the evidence.

courses.title: What a Reading Can Do
courses.blurb: Four non-magical mechanisms with real evidence — projection, re-narration, productive randomness, ritual — and the stance that unlocks them.

courses.title: Tarot Today
courses.blurb: A living question: why a card game is having a cultural moment, read against the data.

courses.title: Working with Claude Desktop
courses.blurb: The powerful path for contributors: GitHub and git from zero, the session habits that keep work safe, and a private-folder pattern.
